brand.full: Gevher Nesibe Healing House and Medical School
brand.tagline: 800 Years of Legacy: The World’s First Medical Faculty and Healing Center.

hero.title: A Scientific Journey Rooted in the Will of Gevher Nesibe Sultan.
hero.lead: The pinnacle of Seljuk civilization: established in Kayseri in 1206, a unique heritage combining medical education and treatment under one roof. Where music, water, and compassion turn into healing.

items.title: The First Medical Faculty
items.body: The world's first institutional example where theoretical education (Medrese) and practical application (Hospital) were conducted together.

items.title: Holistic Healing
items.body: A vision where patients were healed not only physically but also spiritually, blending music, water, and herbal medicine.

items.title: Endowment Culture
items.body: A humanitarian approach offering free service to everyone without discrimination, as per the will of Gevher Nesibe Sultan.

video.title: History of the Complex
video.note: The introductory video for the Kayseri Gevher Nesibe History of Medicine Museum will be added.

posters.title: Historical Documents and Drawings
posters.note: A special collection including architectural plans of the complex, Seljuk medical instruments, and treatment scenes.

historicalContext.title: Selçuklu Science World and STEM
historicalContext.body: The observation, recording, and measurement techniques used 800 years ago at Gevher Nesibe form the basis of today's modern medical disciplines. As part of our 'Cultural Bridge from Roots to Future' project, we re-interpret this ancient heritage through STEM disciplines (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics).

carousel.title: The Last Will of Princess Gevher Nesibe
carousel.description: On her deathbed, she requested her brother to build a place that would provide cure and education for all, leading to the birth of this center.

carousel.title: The Power of Sound and Water
carousel.description: 800 years ago, music therapy and the soothing sound of water were integrated into clinical treatments for psychological disorders.

carousel.title: The First Medical University
carousel.description: The 'Double Madrasa' layout integrated clinical practice and theoretical education under one roof, a first in medical history.

historyPage.title: History & Legacy
historyPage.subtitle: The Story of Gevher Nesibe Sultan, Daughter of Kılıç Arslan
historyPage.intro: Gevher Nesibe Sultan, daughter of Seljuk Sultan Kılıç Arslan II, changed the world of medicine with her will. Upon her death, her brother Gıyaseddin Keyhüsrev I built this 'Double Medrese' to keep her name alive.
bullets: 1204-1206: Construction and commissioning of the Healing House (Hospital).
1210-1214: Completion of the Gıyasiye Medrese (Medical School).
800-Year Timeline: Serving continuously as the oldest and largest medical center in Anatolia.
Tomb of Gevher Nesibe: The eternal resting place of the Sultan located at the heart of the complex.
historyPage.task: What is the greatest similarity between the Seljuk understanding of 'Healing-Service' and modern medical ethics?
historyPage.taskHint: Hint: Consider the dignity of the patient, the right to free treatment, and the master-apprentice discipline of physicians.
historyPage.stem: Science + History of Science: The Seljuks' plant catalogs in pharmacology (pharmacy) are a product of systematic observation that inspires today's biotechnology.
historyPage.bridgeTitle: A Bridge from Yesterday to Today
historyPage.bridge: The 'Tımarhane' (Mental Health) records kept during the Seljuk period are now accepted as the first clinical case files of modern psychiatry. This tradition of data keeping is the foundation of today's big data medicine.
bibliography: Nil Sarı, 'Gevher Nesibe Sultan and Her Healing House', Turkish Archives of Medical History.
Ahmed Süheyl Ünver, 'Medicine in the Seljuk Era', Turkish Historical Society Publications.
Exhibition Data from the Kayseri Seljuk Civilization Museum (Gevher Nesibe Complex).
Osman Turan, 'Turkey in the Time of the Seljuks', Otuken Publishing.

hospitalPage.title: Architecture & Education
hospitalPage.subtitle: The Double Medrese: Where Science and Healing Intertwine
hospitalPage.intro: Architecturally, the complex consists of two main sections connected by a corridor. This structure is tangible proof of Seljuk engineering and educational vision.

sections.title: Shifahiye (Hospital)
sections.body: Located in the western part, this section contains 24 rooms. Patients were divided into different wards based on their ailments, with specialized treatments applied to each.

sections.title: Gıyasiye (Medrese)
sections.body: The medical school located in the eastern part. Students read the works of Galen, Avicenna (Ibn Sina), and Rhazes (Razi), and practiced their theoretical knowledge in the adjacent building.

sections.title: Acoustics and Light
sections.body: The layout of the structure was designed with genius acoustics to maximize sunlight and ensure the sound of water reached every room.

hospitalPage.disclaimer: This information is based on data from the Kayseri Seljuk Civilization Museum (Gevher Nesibe Complex).

casesPage.title: Healing Arts
casesPage.subtitle: Recovery through Music, Water, and Compassion
casesPage.intro: Seljuk medicine views the human not just as a body but as a whole with its soul. The following methods represent the scientific treatment techniques applied at Gevher Nesibe in the 13th century.

internationalPage.title: Scientific Heritage
internationalPage.subtitle: The Science Magnet of the Medieval World
internationalPage.intro: At the time of its establishment, Gevher Nesibe was a scientific center that hosted scholars not only from Anatolia but also from the Middle East and Europe.
steps: Meeting of Different Cultures: The fusion of Arabic, Persian, and Greek medical traditions in the Seljuk crucible.
Traveling Physicians: Doctors trained in Kayseri carried knowledge to centers like Damascus, Baghdad, and Cairo.
Translation Activities: Bringing ancient medical works into Seljuk Turkish and Arabic.
Impact on Modern Medicine: The influence of Seljuk hospital models on the establishment of Europe's medical faculties.

faqs.q: Was surgery performed at Gevher Nesibe?
faqs.a: Yes, 13th-century surgical instruments (scalpels, forceps) displayed in the museum prove that eye and internal disease surgeries were performed.

faqs.q: How long did the education take?
faqs.a: While the duration of education depended on the student's ability, it generally varied between 5-7 years within a master-apprentice relationship.

internationalPage.disclaimer: This section is for general historical information; consult medical history sources for academic research.

contactPage.title: Visit & Contact
contactPage.subtitle: See the Complex Today
contactPage.intro: The Gevher Nesibe Complex, serving today as the Seljuk Civilization Museum, awaits visitors in the heart of Kayseri.

Entrance: Entry with MuseumCard and official tickets.

stem.title: Scientific Heritage STEM
stem.subtitle: Cultural Bridge from Roots to Future

activity.theme: Scientific Heritage STEM - Cultural Bridge from Roots to Future
activity.purpose: This activity invites students to build a 'cultural bridge' between the scientific heritage of the past and the technological world of the future.
activity.maarif: In line with the Turkey Century Education Model; it aims to bring values education, holistic learning, interdisciplinary approach, and skill-based education into the field.

durak2.title: Station 2: History of Health and Science
durak2.description: The Gevher Nesibe Healing House is a unique center where medicine was placed on a scientific basis and the tradition of observation and recording was initiated during the Seljuk period.
durak2.mission: The healing wisdom of the past sheds light on today's modern medical technology.
durak2.maarifModel: Critical thinking and cultural awareness are the basis of our project.

details.methods: In addition to surgical operations, 'Holistic Treatment' was applied 800 years ago using music (makam), water sounds, and herbal remedies. This is a precursor to the psychiatric treatments of that period.
details.science: Seljuk medicine is built on 'observation and experiment'. Under the guidance of the works of Avicenna and Rhazes, specific diet and makam prescriptions were prepared for patients, and all symptoms were meticulously recorded.
details.connection: The foundations of today's modern psychiatric records and plant-based drug (pharmacology) technologies are a continuation of this deep-rooted Seljuk tradition of data keeping and research.

tasks: The Healing House is both a medical school and an application center.
Treatment with music and water sounds is a first in the world.
The tradition of scientific recording is the basis of modern medicine.

conclusion.title: Legacy for the Future
conclusion.body: Gevher Nesibe's legacy is not just made of stone buildings. It is a torch of science passed down through generations, etched into the collective memory of humanity. Today, as the Sirius Team, we take up this torch and bridge the ancient healing knowledge of the Seljuks with today's technology.
